title: Muchhal Mahavir Temple
tagline: Muchhal Mahavir Temple — spiritual town of Rajasthan

overview.short: Muchhal Mahavir temple is a Śvetāmbara Jain temple dedicated to Lord Mahavir, at Ghanerao, in Pali district in Rajasthan state in India.
overview.description: Muchhal Mahavir temple is a Śvetāmbara Jain temple dedicated to Lord Mahavir, at Ghanerao, in Pali district in Rajasthan state in India. The place is on the route from Falna to Kumbhalgarh. The fair is held here every year on the thirteenth day of the month of Chaitra. Muchhal Mahavir temple along with Ranakpur, Narlai, Nadol and Varkana forms "Gorwad Panch Tirth".

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Ghanerao
topPlaces.description: Ghanerao is a village in Desuri tehsil of Pali district of Rajasthan. The village is located on the Sadri-Desuri road State Highway 16.

topPlaces.name: Kumbhalgarh
topPlaces.description: Kumbhalgarh also known as the Great Wall of India, is a fortress located on the western range of the Aravalli Hills in Kumbhalgarh, Rajsamand district, Rajasthan, India. Located approximately 48 km (30 mi) from Rajsamand and 84 km (52 mi) from Udaipur, the fort was constructed in the 15th century by Rana Kumbha.

topPlaces.name: Parshuram Mahadev Temple
topPlaces.description: Parshuram Mahadev Temple is a cave Shiva temple located at border of Pali district and Rajsamand district of Rajasthan state in India. The main cave temple comes in Rajsamand district, whereas Kund Dham comes in Desuri Tehsil of Pali district.

topPlaces.name: Kumbhalgarh Wildlife Sanctuary
topPlaces.description: Kumbhalgarh Wildlife Sanctuary is located in the Rajsamand District of Rajasthan State in western India. It surrounds the Kumbhalgarh Fortress and covers an area of 610.528 km2 (236 sq mi).

topPlaces.name: Desuri
topPlaces.description: Desuri is a Tehsil headquarter, located in the Pali district of Rajasthan, India.

items.note: Ghanerao is a village in Desuri tehsil of Pali district of Rajasthan. The village is located on the Sadri-Desuri road State Highway 16.

Kumbhalgarh also known as the Great Wall of India, is a fortress located on the western range of the Aravalli Hills in Kumbhalgarh, Rajsamand district, Rajasthan, India. Located approximately 48 km (30 mi) from Rajsamand and 84 km (52 mi) from Udaipur, the fort was constructed in the 15th century by Rana Kumbha.

Parshuram Mahadev Temple is a cave Shiva temple located at border of Pali district and Rajsamand district of Rajasthan state in India. The main cave temple comes in Rajsamand district, whereas Kund Dham comes in Desuri Tehsil of Pali district.
Kumbhalgarh Wildlife Sanctuary is located in the Rajsamand District of Rajasthan State in western India. It surrounds the Kumbhalgarh Fortress and covers an area of 610.528 km2 (236 sq mi).
Desuri is a Tehsil headquarter, located in the Pali district of Rajasthan, India.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Muchhal Mahavir Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Muchhal Mahavir Temple is Oct – Mar. Summers are 28–44°C and winters are 8–25°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Muchhal Mahavir Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Maharana Pratap Airport, Udaipur (73 km) and the nearest railway station is Udaipur City (70 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Muchhal Mahavir Temple?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Muchhal Mahavir Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Muchhal Mahavir Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Muchhal Mahavir Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Muchhal Mahavir Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Ghanerao, Kumbhalgarh, Parshuram Mahadev Temple, Kumbhalgarh Wildlife Sanctuary, Desuri.
